# GitHub Copilot Instructions â€” shadcnui-foundry

## Project Overview

shadcnui-foundry is a TypeScript monorepo that builds a multi-framework component generation pipeline.
It ingests component definitions from the shadcn/ui registry, normalizes them into a framework-neutral
intermediate representation (IR), then generates idiomatic implementations for React, Vue, Svelte, Angular, and Lit.

## Architecture Summary

```
Ingest â†’ Analyze â†’ Transform â†’ Emit â†’ Validate
```

| Stage     | Package                | Responsibility                                |
|-----------|------------------------|-----------------------------------------------|
| Ingest    | `@shadcnui-foundry/registry-ingest`  | Fetch raw artifacts from shadcn/ui registry   |
| Analyze   | `@shadcnui-foundry/analyzer`         | Normalize raw artifacts â†’ ComponentIR         |
| Transform | `@shadcnui-foundry/{react,vue,...}`  | Adapt IR for a specific framework             |
| Emit      | `@shadcnui-foundry/{react,vue,...}`  | Generate source file content                  |
| Validate  | `@shadcnui-foundry/a11y-rules`       | Check accessibility rules on IR               |

## Package Dependency Rules

- `ir` has no internal dependencies (only `zod`)
- `core` depends only on `ir`
- `registry-ingest`, `analyzer`, framework packages depend on `core` and `ir`
- `cli` depends on all pipeline packages
- Apps depend on framework packages only

## TypeScript Guidelines

- Use strict TypeScript with `exactOptionalPropertyTypes`
- Use Zod schemas for all external data validation
- Use `PipelineResult<T>` for all stage outputs (never throw from pipeline stages)
- Use `type` imports where possible: `import type { Foo } from "./foo.js"`
- Always include `.js` extensions in relative imports (ESM)
- No `any` â€” use `unknown` and narrow explicitly
- No `!` non-null assertions in production code

## Generated Code Conventions

All generated files must include:
1. A header comment: `// @generated by shadcnui-foundry â€” DO NOT EDIT MANUALLY`
2. A `// Run \`foundry generate <component>\` to regenerate` instruction
3. `irHash` field on `GeneratedFile` set to the SHA-256 of the source IR

Do NOT silently overwrite existing files â€” check before writing and prompt for confirmation.

## Testing

- Unit tests live alongside source in `src/**/*.test.ts`
- Use `createTestComponentIR()` from `@shadcnui-foundry/test-utils` for fixtures
- Use `expectSuccess()` / `expectFailure()` helpers for PipelineResult assertions
- Test filenames: `*.test.ts` (never `*.spec.ts` in this repo)
- Run: `pnpm test` or `pnpm --filter <package> test`

## MCP Integration Points

- MCP server config lives in `.foundry/mcp.json`
- Use `ShadcnRegistryIngester` with `offlineMode: true` when MCP is unavailable
- Cache all fetched artifacts in `.foundry/cache/` with SHA-256 provenance tracking
- Never make MCP calls from within library packages â€” only from CLI commands

## Commit Guidelines

- Use conventional commits: `feat:`, `fix:`, `docs:`, `refactor:`, `test:`, `ci:`, `gen:`, `chore:`
- Scope to the package: `feat(ir): add union prop type support`
- Generated code commits: use `gen:` type

## Agent Skills (Operational)

Use the Agent Skills-formatted files in `agent-skills/`:

- `agent-skills/fast-local-validation/SKILL.md`
- `agent-skills/component-generation-flow/SKILL.md`
- `agent-skills/storybook-playground-parity/SKILL.md`
- `agent-skills/safe-generated-file-updates/SKILL.md`
- `agent-skills/framework-parity-checks/SKILL.md`

These skills use YAML frontmatter + markdown instructions per the Agent Skills specification.
